preamble: In Bristol English and related West Country dialects, a final [l] is inserted after open/schwa-like vowels including [ɛ] in certain phonetic environments. Note: this file documents the specific context of [l]-insertion after open-mid front [ɛ], a more restricted version of the general Bristol L (see empty_to_l).

examples.note: Bristol L: final [l] inserted after open vowel [ɛ] in vowel-final words. The same phenomenon applies after schwa.
